violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the vent cover of the walk-in have an accumulation of dust. Cover to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, some foods have been in unit since yesterday. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Water was added to quaternary ammonium solution to reach adequate ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
Both prep units were unable to keep temperature. Both units were observed uncovered. Units were adjusted. Units to be serviced by technician. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

Sliced tomatoes and cut leafy greens were observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, food had been placed on ice no longer than two hours. PIC instructed to move TCS foods off ice and place in refrigeration unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

The wall behind the prep unit consists of red brick. Except in areas used only for dry storage, concrete, porous blocks, or bricks used for indoor wall construction shall be finished and SEALED to provide a SMOOTH, nonabsorbent, EASILY CLEANABLE surface. New Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2023
